fixture: 069_scalar_string_functions

statement 1:
  sql:
    SELECT
      UPPER('tucotuco') AS upper_text,
      LOWER('TUCOTUCO') AS lower_text,
      TRIM(FROM '  tucotuco  ') AS trimmed_text,
      TRIM(LEADING '.' FROM '..tucotuco') AS left_trimmed,
      TRIM(TRAILING '.' FROM 'tucotuco..') AS right_trimmed,
      SUBSTRING('tucotuco' FROM 2 FOR 4) AS slice_text,
      POSITION('tu' IN 'tucotuco') AS first_pos,
      CHAR_LENGTH('tucotuco') AS char_len,
      CHARACTER_LENGTH('tucotuco') AS character_len,
      OCTET_LENGTH('tucotuco') AS octet_len,
      OVERLAY('abcdef' PLACING 'ZZ' FROM 2 FOR 3) AS overlay_text,
      CONCAT('sql', '') AS concat_text
  result:
    query
    columns:
      [0] upper_text VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL
      [1] lower_text VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL
      [2] trimmed_text VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL
      [3] left_trimmed VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL
      [4] right_trimmed VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL
      [5] slice_text VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL
      [6] first_pos BIGINT NOT NULL
      [7] char_len BIGINT NOT NULL
      [8] character_len BIGINT NOT NULL
      [9] octet_len BIGINT NOT NULL
      [10] overlay_text VARCHAR(6) NOT NULL
      [11] concat_text VARCHAR(3) NOT NULL
    rows:
      [0] "TUCOTUCO" | "tucotuco" | "tucotuco" | "tucotuco" | "tucotuco" | "ucot" | 1 | 8 | 8 | 8 | "aZZef" | "sql"

statement 2:
  sql:
    SELECT
      REGEXP_LIKE('abc123', '[0-9]+') AS has_digits,
      REGEXP_REPLACE('abc123', '[0-9]+', 'x') AS stripped_text,
      REGEXP_SUBSTR('abc123', '[0-9]+') AS matched_digits
  result:
    query
    columns:
      [0] has_digits BOOLEAN NOT NULL
      [1] stripped_text VARCHAR(6) NOT NULL
      [2] matched_digits VARCHAR(6) NOT NULL
    rows:
      [0] true | "abcx" | "123"
